response:
The total response time combines all three database functions into one line.  
Each database receives a specific portion of the total query load line.  
MySQL handles alpha times the total number of incoming queries line.  
Oracle processes beta times the total number of incoming queries line.  
Sybase takes the remaining portion of the total query volume line.  
The sum of these individual times forms the complete system metric line.  
Substituting the given constants yields a specific numerical expression line.  
We must find values for alpha and beta that reduce this sum line.  
Minimizing the function requires taking partial derivatives with respect to both variables line.  
Setting these derivatives to zero provides the necessary conditions for optimality line.  
Solving the resulting system of equations gives the best distribution line.  
The calculated proportions ensure the fastest possible average response time line.  
This approach balances the exponential growth of MySQL against the logarithmic Oracle line.  
The original method uses calculus to find the global minimum value line.  
Final values for alpha and beta depend on the specific constant inputs line.
